title: Xantolis racemosa
taxonomy: Xantolis racemosa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is native to Asia, specifically in countries such as India and China. This plant is a part of the genus Xantolis, which is a group of plants that are closely related to the genus Pouteria. Xantolis racemosa is a type of evergreen tree or shrub that is often found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Xantolis racemosa is further divided into various subcategories, including its binomial nomenclature.
uses: Xantolis racemosa has been traditionally used in various medicinal applications. The plant's leaves and roots are valued for their potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. In traditional medicine, Xantolis racemosa is used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and skin conditions. Its extracts are also used as a remedy for respiratory problems and as an antacid. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and potential side effects of using Xantolis racemosa for medicinal purposes.

It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
